message:
fix(viewer): prevent phantom detail pane from hiding graph nodes

The graph detail pane (x-show="graphDetailNode") was appearing on
page load even though graphDetailNode was null. This happened because
Alpine's :style binding (which set top positioning as a string)
replaced the entire inline style attribute, clobbering the
display:none that x-show should have set. The pane rendered with
default values ("P2", "Task", "No description") and on mobile
covered 60% of the viewport as a bottom sheet, completely obscuring
all graph nodes — matching the reported bug of "just controls, no
nodes."

- Remove :style binding from detail pane, move top:64px to Tailwind
  class md:top-[64px] so x-show can manage display unimpeded
- Add style="display:none" to graph loading overlay as defense
  against same nested x-show initialization timing issue

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2896,6 +2896,7 @@
100) || (!isMobile && swipeX > 100)) { graphDetailNode = null; if(forceGraphModule?.clearSelection) forceGraphModule.clearSelection(); setTimeout(() => resizeForceGraph(), 250); } $el.style.transform = ''; swiping = false; }" class="fixed z-50 flex flex-col touch-pan-y bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 shadow-2xl overflow-hidden bottom-0 left-0 right-0 h-[60vh] rounded-t-2xl border-t border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700 - md:bottom-auto md:left-auto md:right-0 md:top-0 md:w-[400px] md:h-full md:rounded-none md:border-l md:border-t-0" - :style="'top: ' + (isMobile ? 'auto' : '64px')"> + md:bottom-auto md:left-auto md:right-0 md:top-[64px] md:w-[400px] md:h-[calc(100vh-64px)] md:rounded-none md:border-l md:border-t-0">
